summary: Improvement cycle addressing external review #1 from berendt. C-001 (bootstrap Python pre-insert script reading OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION before falling back to configparser) fixed with regression test TestBuildBootstrapJob_PreInsertScriptReadsDBConnectionEnvVar. W-001 (brittle substring match) fixed by introducing secrets.ErrKeyNotFound sentinel and switching to errors.Is. W-002 (docs function-name drift) fixed in keystone-reconciler.md. I-001 (dbConnectionSecretKey constant adoption) applied across unit/integration tests with chainsaw YAML carrying a sync comment. I-002 godoc reworded. All tests pass; go build and go vet clean.

tests_checklist.item: C-001 regression test asserts env-var lookup precedes configparser fallback
W-001 regression test asserts errors.Is(err, ErrKeyNotFound) wrapping

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (sentinel-error convention, constant reuse)
Comments explain WHY (placeholder/DNS failure, oslo.config override)
No dead code introduced; strings import removed after substring match replaced with errors.Is

security_checklist.item: Derived Secret still holds the connection URL (no password leakage to ConfigMap)
Bootstrap Job still sources DB URL from SecretKeyRef, never from literal

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (sentinel + errors.Is replaces substring match)
Env-var-first-then-config fallback mirrors how oslo.config resolves overrides

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code; dbConnectionSecretKey constant reused across Go call sites
Chainsaw YAML retains literal 'connection' with sync comment (unavoidable cross-language boundary)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Missing-key errors now carry typed sentinel so callers cannot accidentally swallow them
Bootstrap script fallback still produces a failure if env var unset (placeholder URL unresolvable)

defensive_checklist.item: errors.Is walks wrap chain; substring drift eliminated
Bootstrap script defensively guards against absent OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION

suggested_improvements: Consider exporting dbConnectionSecretKey (or a shared constant in a types package) so chainsaw fixtures generated from Go templates could reference it programmatically — low priority since Chainsaw YAML is hand-written.

The bootstrap script's configparser fallback is defensive but unreachable in practice (env var is always wired via SecretKeyRef; if the Secret is missing the pod won't start). A follow-up could remove the fallback to simplify the script, but keeping it as a belt-and-braces guard is also defensible.
next_steps: Merge once CI (go test, go vet, golangci-lint, gosec, chainsaw) is green.
Consider running the configmap-no-secrets and basic-deployment chainsaw suites as a post-merge sanity check that the env-var-first bootstrap script reaches the DB.
